edge_hypothesis: EMA 9/21 crossover is the simplest trend signal — and simplicity may be the edge. Existing EMA crossover specs use 3:1-3.5:1 R:R ratios. This spec pushes the R:R thesis to its logical extreme: 4:1 to 6:1 R:R with a transitional-regime EXCLUSION gate. Advisory data proves transitional regimes are universally toxic (PF=0.858 on ETH/1h, worst regime across all assets). By explicitly blocking trades when ADX is in the 15-25 'no man's land', we eliminate the lowest-quality 20-30% of signals. The remaining signals occur in either strong trends (ADX>25, where crossovers mark genuine trend shifts) or deep ranges (ADX<15, where crossovers catch range extremes). This is NOT about adding complexity — it's about SUBTRACTING the worst signals via regime exclusion.
regime_assumption: Targets two distinct regimes with different mechanics: (1) Strong trending (ADX>25): EMA crossover marks genuine trend acceleration, high follow-through expected. (2) Deep ranging (ADX<15): crossover catches range-boundary mean-reversion, shorter hold appropriate. EXCLUDES transitional (ADX 15-25): this is the 'dead zone' where trends haven't committed and ranges haven't formed — the worst regime for every template tested. The standard variant gates trending-only; the dual-regime variant tests both.

variants.name: ema_cross_trending_only
variants.description: EMA 9/21 crossover gated to trending-only (ADX>25). Tests pure trend-following with extreme 4:1 R:R. Advisory shows ETH/4h trending PF=1.054 — this variant isolates trending regime to see if EMA crossover captures that edge. Deliberately excludes transitional trades that killed performance in other templates.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close (bullish crossover).
Require ADX_14 > 25 (strong trend — exclude transitional dead zone).
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close (bearish crossover).
Require ADX_14 > 25 (strong trend — exclude transitional dead zone).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: ADX_14 > 25 (trending only — transitional excluded)
exit_rules: Exit on EMA_9 crossing back through EMA_21 (crossover reversal).
Exit on ADX_14 dropping below 18 (trend dissipating).
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (25 bars = ~4 days on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: ADX>25 from news spike, not sustained trend
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Crossover at trend exhaustion (late entry)
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry (4:1 R:R — extreme fee-drag countermeasure).
Regime exclusion: removing transitional trades should lift PF by eliminating worst 20-30% of signals.

variants.name: ema_cross_extreme_rr
variants.description: EMA 9/21 crossover with maximum 6:1 R:R and moderate ADX gate (>20). Tests whether extremely wide TP targets can overcome fee drag even with lower win rate. Math: at 6:1 R:R, a 17% win rate breaks even. If EMA crossover can achieve >20% win rate in trending conditions, this is profitable. Extended hold (35 bars) lets winners run to extreme TP targets.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Extreme R:R thesis: 6:1 R:R requires only 17% win rate to break even
exit_rules: Exit on EMA_9 crossing back through EMA_21.
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (35 bars = ~6 days on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Price reverses before reaching extreme TP target
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Extended hold causes excessive unrealized-gain drawdown
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry (6:1 R:R — extreme target).
Breakeven math: at 6:1 R:R, only 17% win rate needed. EMA crossover on 4h should exceed this.

variants.name: ema_cross_tight_stop_wide_tp
variants.description: Asymmetric risk profile: tight stop (1.0x ATR) with wide TP (5.0x ATR) = 5:1 R:R. Tests whether cutting losers FAST while letting winners breathe produces better PF than symmetric stops. Tight stop means more frequent stop-outs, but each loss is smaller. The advisory noted 62% fee drag — reducing loss magnitude per trade directly improves net PF. ADX>22 moderate gate.
entry_long: Require EMA_9 crosses above EMA_21 on bar close.
entry_short: Require EMA_9 crosses below EMA_21 on bar close.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Asymmetric risk: tight stop + wide TP to cut losers fast
exit_rules: Exit on EMA_9 crossing back through EMA_21.
Exit on max bars in trade threshold (20 bars = ~3.3 days on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Tight stop causes excessive stop-outs in volatile bars
Hypothesis failure mode guard: Winners don't reach wide TP before time exit
risk_rules: Risk note: risk per trade target is 1%.
Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 from entry (TIGHT — cut losers fast).
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry (5:1 R:R asymmetric profile).
Asymmetric thesis: losing 1 ATR frequently is acceptable if winners capture 5 ATR occasionally.
